LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - MS. BONO INFORMED THE COURT THAT SHE IS EXCERCISING HER CCP 170.6 CHALLENGE TO JUDGE SULLIVAN. THE COURT INSTRUCTED DEFENDANT TO DO SO IN WRITING OR ORALLY UNDER THE PENALTY OF PERJURY. SHE WAS NOT ALLOWED TO EXCERCISE HER CHALLENGE THIS MORNING SINCE SHE WAS APPEARING THROUGH COURT CALL. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT JULIA BONO'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE VOID DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT; TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS - THIS MOTION WILL BE HEARD BEFORE JUDGE SULLIVAN ON OCTOBER 8, 2013, IN DEPARTMENT 514 AT 9:00 A.M. Defendant Julia Bono'S Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-28-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DAVID B. GOLDEN OF GOLDEN & KOPCKE LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 399-9994 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
JULIA BONO, TEL. NO. (707)-987-1061, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Julia Bono's Motion To Set Aside Default/Default Judgment is Granted. Mexico is a party to the Hague Service Convention, and therefore, Plaintiff was required to serve Defendant, a resident of Mexico, pursuant to methods of service the Hague Service Convention authorizes. The Hague Service Convention does not authorize personal service. Mexico objected to service by mail in Article V of its declarations made at the moment of accession (1999). (See also In re Alyssa F. ((2003) 112 Cal.App.4th 846, 854, "service of process by ordinary mail does not perfect service in Mexico.") Failure to comply with Hague procedures voids service, even if it was made in compliance with California law and even if the defendant has actual notice of the suit. (Kott v. Superior Ct. (1996) 45 Cal.App.4th 1126, 1136.) Therefore, service was not properly effectuated on Defendant and the judgment entered against her is void for lack of personal jurisdiction. The default and default judgment are set aside pursuant to CCP 473(d). Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/EHG)

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Jun-03-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

David B. Golden, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Stefan Guistino, who was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendants Carlos Gustavo Buno Rovirosa or Julia Carter De Bono.
Defaults were entered against defendants on September 10, 2009.
Doe defendants were dismissed today, June 3, 2010.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Stefan Giustino, and against the Defaulted Defendants Carlos Gustavo Bona Rovirosa and Julia Carter De Bono as follows:

As to Defendant Carlos Gustavo Bono Rovirosa:
Damages/Principal - $346,000.00.
Prejudgment Interest - $64,701.32.
Costs - $292.50

Total - $410,933.82.

As to Defendant Julia Carter De Bono:
Damages - $20,000.00.
Prejudgment Interest - $6,372.55.
Costs - $292.50.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
